Analysts at the US investment firm noted that, ahead of the electric vehicle maker's delivery numbers, which are due to be released at the end of the week or at the weekend, future estimates will be far more important than actual deliveries, which were hampered by supply chain issues and factory shutdowns in Shanghai.
